mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.1% indicates that monetary policy is extremely accommodative, compressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations. This persistently low rate environment is positive for S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window falls below 0.02, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate from elevated levels signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops below 8.5% after having been above 9.0% at any point in the prior 12 months
failure_condition: When triggered, the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns is less than 55%, or the average return is not significantly greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and retail sales reflects broad-based domestic demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and supporting equity prices over the next month.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of HOUST and RSAFS both show positive growth from 6 months prior
failure_condition: The joint signal (both series rising) does not provide an information coefficient above 0.04 for 20-day returns over a 3-year walk-forward window, or returns under the signal are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: In the early-cycle expansion phase, rising industrial production alongside moderate GDP growth indicates synchronized recovery without overheating, supporting equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth is positive and GDPC1 year-over-year growth is between 1.5% and 3.5%
failure_condition: Fails if 60-day returns during early-cycle expansions (as defined) are negative more than 40% of the time, or the rank IC over any 3-year period is less than 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a threshold raises expectations of tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and depressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When triggered, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment gains combined with a falling unemployment rate signals a robust labor market that boosts aggregate demand and corporate earnings, supporting equity prices over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200K and UNRATE monthly change < -0.1 percentage point
failure_condition: The joint signal fails if 20-day forward returns under this condition are not positive in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the information ratio is below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer spending, which directly drives corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The rank IC for RSAFS as a standalone signal on 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year walk-forward window, or Sharpe ratio of the strategy is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth accelerates relative to GDP, it signals strengthening output capacity and cyclical momentum, which is positive for equities in a post-recession environment.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when triggered are negative more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not statistically different from the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.10).
